title: Enforce Strict mTLS with PeerAuthentication
description:
## Enforce Strict mTLS with PeerAuthentication

Once all services in a namespace are enrolled in the mesh, you should switch from `PERMISSIVE` to `STRICT` mTLS to reject any plain-text traffic and ensure all communication is encrypted.

**Your task:**

Create a `PeerAuthentication` resource named `default` in the `secure-ns` namespace that enforces **STRICT** mTLS for all workloads in that namespace.

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get peerauthentication default -n secure-ns -o jsonpath='{.spec.mtls.mode}'
```

hints.title: PeerAuthentication resource structure
hints.body: Apply a PeerAuthentication in the target namespace. Omitting a selector makes it namespace-wide. Set spec.mtls.mode to STRICT.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: secure-ns
spec:
  mtls:
    mode: STRICT
EOF

validation.description: Check that the PeerAuthentication enforces STRICT mTLS in secure-ns

commands.command: kubectl get peerauthentication default -n secure-ns -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get peerauthentication default -n secure-ns -o jsonpath='{.spec.mtls.mode}'
